text:
Emiliano Catonini
Emiliano Catonini is an Associate Professor of Economics at NYU Shanghai. He graduated with a PhD in Economics from Bocconi University in 2013. From 2013 to 2021, he worked at the International College of Economics and Finance of Higher School of Economics in Moscow. Emiliano researches in the areas of game theory, information economics, and mechanism design. His working papers can be viewed here. His CV can also be viewed here.
Select Publications
Battigalli P. and E. Catonini. Monotonicity and robust implementation under forward-induction reasoning. Econometrica. 2026. Vol. 94, No. 2, P. 505–536.
Catonini E. Iterated Admissibility does not Refine Extensive-form Rationalizability // The Economic Journal. 2024. Vol. 134. P. 3017-3026
Catonini E. Self-enforcing agreements and forward induction reasoning // The Review of Economic Studies. 2021. Vol. 88. P. 610-642
Battigalli P. and E. Catonini. The epistemic spirit of divinity. Journal of Economic Theory. 2024. Vol. 222. 105903.
Catonini E. and S. Stepanov On the optimality of full disclosure. // The RAND Journal of Economics. 2025. 00:1–15

text_blocks.text: Emiliano Catonini is an Associate Professor of Economics at NYU Shanghai. He graduated with a PhD in Economics from Bocconi University in 2013. From 2013 to 2021, he worked at the International College of Economics and Finance of Higher School of Economics in Moscow. Emiliano researches in the areas of game theory, information economics, and mechanism design. His working papers can be viewed here. His CV can also be viewed here.
